description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a cylindrical, slightly bulging body constructed from vertical wooden staves. The staves are held tightly together by two prominent, light-colored bands or hoops encircling the barrel near the top and bottom. The wood appears weathered and aged, with visible grain, knots, and some darkened areas, suggesting prolonged exposure to the elements. The top and bottom surfaces are flat and made from the same wood as the sides, with the top surface showing clear plank divisions. There are no visible handles, spouts, or openings other than the closed top. The overall appearance is rustic and utilitarian, typical of barrels used for storage or transport in urban or industrial settings.
description: A weathered wooden barrel with two light-colored bands, flat plank top, and rustic, aged wood texture.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full cylindrical shape, both bands, and the flat wooden top with visible plank lines.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view revealing the curvature of the staves and the continuity of the bands around the barrel.
description_view_2: Another side view, similar to the front, highlighting the wood grain and the even spacing of the bands.
description_view_3: Back view, nearly identical to the front, with clear visibility of the barrel's bulge and banding.
